summary: preinstall script exfiltrates installer's git identity and OS username to hardcoded remote host
details: The package's `preinstall` lifecycle hook (`dist/scripts/preinstall.js`) fires automatically on `npm install` and performs silent telemetry/exfiltration of installer-side identity data. It reads `~/.gitconfig` via iniparser, extracts `user.name` and `user.email`, reads the OS username from `os.userInfo().username`, and unconditionally transmits them as query parameters in an HTTPS GET to a hardcoded remote host: `https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext?osname=<user>&gitname=<name>&gitemail=<email>`. The destination hostname (`2tak.l.serverhost.name` on `l.serverhost.name`, a dynamic-DNS-style subdomain) does not match the package publisher's identity, the user has not consented to this data collection, and the payload identifies the developer personally. Compounding concern: the `postinstall` script (`dist/scripts/postinstall.js`) rewrites the installed `package.json` stripping `scripts`, `dependencies`, `devDependencies`, and `eslintConfig`, then recursively deletes `./dist/scripts`, `./dist/data`, `./dist/resources`, and `./dist/source` — a self-cleanup pattern that removes the exfiltration script from disk after it has run, obscuring post-install forensics. Installer harm is concrete: personal developer identity silently leaked to a third-party host on every `npm install`, with evidence of the mechanism deleted afterwards.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
